No acronyms used without spell-out. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously. Sentences are direct and action-oriented; no hedging language found. Average sentence length is well under 25 words. 0 soft findings — no escalation threshold triggered.

verdict: PASS. All 6 corpus-critic checks pass with no hard failures and no soft findings. (1) Required sections §3, §4, §5, §10 are all present and non-trivial. (4) Estimated ~3420 words — within the standard profile range of 3000–4400. (5) No scope creep: corpus stays on testing/verifying, does not teach iteration or spec-writing; both worked examples apply the testing process only. (6) Beginner readability is strong: all terms defined on first use, no jargon dumps, no academic register, 0 soft findings.
